name: London
description: The Thames, Tube, towers, and Whitehall — facts about Britain's capital city.

questions.q: Which river flows through the heart of London?

source.notes: 215 miles long, the second-longest river in the UK.

questions.q: What is the address of the British Prime Minister's official residence?

source.notes: 11 Downing Street is the Chancellor's residence.

questions.q: What is the actual name of the Houses of Parliament's famous clock tower (since 2012)?

source.notes: Renamed Elizabeth Tower in honor of QE2's Diamond Jubilee. 'Big Ben' technically refers to the largest of the bells inside.

questions.q: Which palace has been the official London residence of British monarchs since 1837?

source.notes: Queen Victoria was the first to make it the official residence.

questions.q: Which historic Tower of London royal birds — by tradition — must always be present or the kingdom will fall?

source.notes: Six (plus a spare) ravens are kept by the Ravenmaster.

questions.q: Which famous London square hosts Nelson's Column?

source.notes: Commemorates Nelson's victory at the 1805 Battle of Trafalgar.

questions.q: What is the colloquial nickname for the London Underground?
In what year did the London Underground open — making it the world's first underground railway?
Which two-day Caribbean carnival in late August is Europe's largest street festival?

source.notes: Held since 1966 over the August bank holiday.

questions.q: What is the name of the giant Ferris-wheel observation attraction on the South Bank?

source.notes: Opened in 2000 as the Millennium Wheel.

questions.q: Which iconic London bridge raises in the middle to let ships through?

source.notes: Often confused with London Bridge — the plain stone bridge upstream.

questions.q: Which Royal Park is home to Kensington Palace and the Round Pond?

source.notes: Borders Hyde Park to the west.

questions.q: What giant Christopher Wren-designed dome dominates the City of London skyline?
Which year did the Great Fire of London destroy much of the medieval city?

source.notes: Started in a baker's shop on Pudding Lane.

questions.q: Which abbey has been the coronation site of every English/British monarch since 1066?

source.notes: Coronation Chair has been there since 1296.

questions.q: What is the name of London's prehistoric stone-clad financial district?

source.notes: The City of London (or simply 'the City') is also nicknamed 'the Square Mile.'

questions.q: Which underground line is colored red on Tube maps and connects Stratford to Ealing Broadway?

source.notes: Red since the original 1908 colour scheme.

questions.q: Which London airport is the busiest by passenger numbers?

source.notes: LHR — busiest airport in Europe by international traffic.

questions.q: Which famous London market in East London is known for vintage clothing and fabrics?

source.notes: Brick Lane market is also known for its curry houses.

questions.q: What does the Cockney rhyming-slang phrase 'mince pies' mean?
